relevant_files.role: Workspace manifest; defines [workspace.dependencies] where tree-sitter-kotlin must be added
Crate manifest; defines [features] and optional deps; lang-kotlin feature and dep go here
EXTENSION_MAP (.kt/.kts -> kotlin) and supported_languages() list; both need kotlin entries
get_language_info() and get_ts_language() match arms; mod kotlin; declaration all needed
New language module: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, optional DEFUSE_QUERY, extract_inheritance, unit tests
Closest template for Kotlin (JVM-style AST, inheritance, DEFUSE_QUERY pattern)
Example of defuse_query: None (Fortran omits it); illustrates omission precedent
Integration-level semantic correctness tests per language; kotlin test would follow java pattern here

conventions.commits: Conventional Commits (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor/test) with scope; GPG-signed (-S) and DCO signed-off (--signoff); example: 'feat(lang): add Kotlin grammar support'
conventions.testing: Unit tests co-located in language module under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-kotlin"))]; AAA pattern; parse_kotlin() helper; one happy path + one edge case per behavior; integration semantic-correctness test in crates/code-analyze-core/tests/semantic_correctness.rs
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --profile ci -- -D warnings -W clippy::cognitive_complexity, cargo deny check advisories licenses; SPDX header required on all .rs files
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for library errors; no .unwrap() in production paths; Option returns via None for unsupported/unknown

patterns: Feature-gated language support via #[cfg(feature = "lang-X")] on every touch point: EXTENSION_MAP entries, supported_languages() slice, mod declaration, get_language_info() arm, get_ts_language() arm

LanguageInfo struct with fixed field set: name, language, element_query, call_query, reference_query, import_query, impl_query, impl_trait_query, defuse_query, extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type, extract_inheritance -- all optional fields set to None when not applicable
Language crate entry point is LANGUAGE const (e.g. tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE.into())
Query constants as pub const &str in the language module; named: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY
extract_inheritance as pub fn(node: &Node, source: &str) -> Vec<String> with #[must_use]
Two-line SPDX header at top of every new .rs file
Optional features declared as dep:tree-sitter-X in workspace Cargo.toml; optional = true in crate Cargo.toml
defuse_query set to None for Fortran (precedent); all other languages include DEFUSE_QUERY

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add Kotlin grammar support
related_issues.relevance: The target issue; fully specifies acceptance criteria, node kinds, query patterns, inheritance walk, and what is out of scope

related_issues.title: feat(core): def-use query infrastructure and write/read site extraction (PR)
related_issues.relevance: Introduced defuse_query field on LanguageInfo and DEFUSE_QUERY pattern for 8 languages; kotlin.rs must include or explicitly omit this field

related_issues.title: feat(languages): add Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Java, Kotlin, Swift, Ruby modules (MERGED)
related_issues.relevance: Kotlin was previously added then removed (PR #63); suggests the grammar was once integrated and later dropped as unused

related_issues.title: chore(languages): drop unused grammars (kotlin, swift, ruby, javascript) (MERGED)
related_issues.relevance: Kotlin grammar was removed in this cleanup; any surviving patterns from that era may differ from current conventions

constraints: tree-sitter-kotlin version must be exactly 0.3.8 per issue acceptance criteria
tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 license is MIT; allowed by deny.toml
CI coverage thresholds: 90% lines / 80% regions for code-analyze-core; new tests must not drop below these
SPDX-FileCopyrightText and SPDX-License-Identifier headers required on all new .rs files
cargo deny check advisories licenses must pass (MIT is in the allow list)
Kotlin 0.3.8 crate entry point must be verified; training-data assumption that LANGUAGE const exists needs smoke-test before commit
test_coverage: Each language module carries its own unit tests under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-X"))]. Java (the closest analog) has 3 focused unit tests: ELEMENT_QUERY happy path, extract_inheritance, and defuse write-site. CI gates on 90% line coverage via cargo-llvm-cov with cargo-nextest. The issue specifies 11 unit tests for kotlin.rs covering: free function, method-in-class, call, class, data class, interface, enum class, import, single inheritance (superclass with parens), multiple interface conformance (no parens), and mixed delegation specifiers.

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE (LanguageFn const, .into() yields tree_sitter::Language); consistent with all other language crates in the workspace
library_findings.notes: Published on crates.io by fwcd; MIT license; confirmed present on crates.io via cargo search and cargo info; not yet in Cargo.lock (not a current dep). Key node kinds per issue: function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration for elements; call_expression for calls; import_header for imports; delegation_specifiers for inheritance walk. Companion objects use object_declaration with companion keyword modifier.

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter::Language (via .into() from LanguageFn), Query::new(), QueryCursor, Node, StreamingIterator pattern for cursor.matches()
library_findings.notes: Workspace pins tree-sitter = 0.26.6. ABI version must match between tree-sitter and tree-sitter-kotlin; 0.3.8 of tree-sitter-kotlin targets tree-sitter 0.22-0.24 ABI historically -- this must be verified with cargo check before PR.

approaches.name: Minimal -- no DEFUSE_QUERY (parity with Fortran)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace deps and crate features. Create kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, and extract_inheritance. Set defuse_query: None in the LanguageInfo arm (matching Fortran). Wire up lang.rs (.kt, .kts), languages/mod.rs (mod, get_language_info, get_ts_language). Unit tests cover the 11 behaviors from the issue. README table updated.
pros: Simpler -- no need to research Kotlin-specific write/read AST node kinds
Consistent with Fortran precedent for languages where def-use semantics are complex
Avoids a query that could produce false positives before node kinds are verified
cons: Does not provide def-use site extraction for Kotlin; analyze_symbol with def_use=true returns no sites
Issue acceptance criteria does not explicitly require DEFUSE_QUERY; but every other JVM language (Java) has one
Slightly weaker feature parity with Java, the closest analog

approaches.name: Full -- with DEFUSE_QUERY modeled after Java
approaches.description: Same as the minimal approach, plus a DEFUSE_QUERY constant in kotlin.rs that mirrors Java's pattern: property_declaration for write sites (Kotlin uses val/var), assignment_expression for LHS writes, augmented_assignment_expression for write-read, and identifier for reads. Requires verifying actual node kinds emitted by tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 for val/var declarations. Set defuse_query: Some(kotlin::DEFUSE_QUERY) in get_language_info(). Add a defuse write-site unit test.
pros: Full feature parity with Java and all other current JVM/OO languages
analyze_symbol def_use=true works for Kotlin files immediately
Matches the pattern established by PR #659 for completeness
cons: Requires verifying Kotlin-specific node kind names for val/var declarations against 0.3.8 grammar (risk of wrong node kind names causing a panicking query compile error)
One additional test case and one more query constant; modest but real extra complexity
If node kinds differ from assumptions, runtime Query::new() will return an error that surfaces as a panic in the query cache; must smoke-test

approaches.name: Full with semantic-correctness integration test
approaches.description: Same as the full approach, plus a test in crates/code-analyze-core/tests/semantic_correctness.rs following the Java pattern: write a .kt temp file with a class, method, and import, run analyze_file(), assert on semantic.functions, semantic.classes, and semantic.imports counts. This mirrors how every other language is exercised at the integration layer.
pros: Closes the integration coverage gap; the issue does not forbid adding to semantic_correctness.rs but Java sets the pattern
Guards against regression if the grammar ABI shifts in a future upgrade
Keeps coverage above CI thresholds more comfortably
cons: One more file touched (semantic_correctness.rs) beyond the 5 core files
If tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 has a runtime ABI mismatch with tree-sitter 0.26.6, this test will fail at runtime not compile time -- surfacing the issue early is actually a pro

recommendation: Approach 3 (full with semantic-correctness integration test). The issue specifies 11 unit tests and Java as the reference template; Java includes DEFUSE_QUERY and a semantic_correctness integration test. Kotlin is a JVM language with nearly identical AST structure (val/var declarations map to local_variable_declaration-equivalent nodes). DEFUSE_QUERY for Kotlin is low-risk to add with node kind verification via cargo check. The integration test adds a meaningful regression guard and mirrors the existing pattern for every other supported language. The only prerequisite is confirming tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 ABI compatibility with tree-sitter 0.26.6 via cargo check before finalizing.
